headline: Has The Recent Pullback Opened An Opportunity In Colgate-Palmolive (CL)?
summary: If you are wondering whether Colgate-Palmolive at around US$85.12 is still a solid entry or already pricing in too much optimism, the next sections will walk through what the numbers are actually saying about value. The share price has pulled back recently, with a 4.2% decline over the last week and a 10.5% decline over the last month, even though the year to date return sits at 9.6% and the 3 year and 5 year returns are 24.5% and 20.9% respectively. headline: Spike in oil prices seen as ’a clear risk for consumer equities’
summary: Investing.com — A sharp rise in oil prices amid the escalating Iran conflict is emerging as a clear risk for consumer equities, with analysts warning that the biggest threat lies in margins rather than immediate demand destruction.

headline: Why this is not 2022 for the Euro
summary: Investing.com -- The Euro has demonstrated a surprising degree of stability over the past week, defying a broader shift in sentiment that has seen it singled out for selling within the G10 currency basket. According to a new analysis from BofA Global Research, the common currency has faced pressure in near-dated options, but its spot performance remains anchored by the continued stability of European natural gas prices.
